content.full_text:
TO: Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

ATTN: Deanne Stone - Legal Division

DATE: May 26, 1992

FROM: Elsinore-Murrieta-Anza Resource Conservation District
Attn: Robert Wheeler, Director & Board President
H.C. 1, Box 360
Anza, CA 92539

RE: Case #92-312 (incompatibility of offices)

You recently received (letter dated 5-18-92) from
Melba Hoffman, Administrative Aide to Riverside County
Supervisor Ceniceros, an inquiry concerning incompatibility
of office.

The first case has to do with my own tenure on
the Anza Valley Municipal Advisory Council, on which
I have served for over four years and long prior to coming
to the Board of the Resource Conservation District.
Anza Valley is within and a part of the Resource
Conservation District area, and because the AVMAC deals
with a great breadth of subject matter, has in my view
a substantial potential for overlap of and conflicts between
policy positions. Please see the enclosed information
defining both AVMAC's and the RCD's. Having called this
to the County's attention, I will appreciate your comments
upon my concern.

The second case has to do with a person who has
for a number of years held a position as a Flood Control
District (Zone 7) Commissioner - one of the Zone 7 Board
which is advisory to the Riverside County Flood Control
District in our area. The person filed an application
for a position on the Board of Directors of our Resource
Conservation District.

Geographically, the RCD generally occupies the
same area as Zone 7 of the Flood Control District. The
subject matter of the two districts also substantially
overlaps. For your assistance I have enclosed a copy
of the mission statement for Riverside County Flood along
with information for the RCD.

Historically, the E-M-A/RCD has taken, along with

CONSERVATION · DEVELOPMENT · SELF-GOVERNMENT

JUN 2 1992

our technical backup agency, the U.S. Soil Conservation
Service, a strong conservation position. On the other
hand, Flood - as expressed on several occasions by their
Chief Engineer Ken Edwards - has taken the position that
Flood's concern begins "when the water hits the ditch"
and not before. This has led over the years to a conflict
between Flood, who want to channelize large stretches
within the Santa Margarita River System, and the RCD,
SCS, Army Corps of Engineers, U.S. EPA, U.S. Fish &
Wildlife, and others, who want to conserve the waters
through approaches that are alternative and in contrast
to channelization. The conflict of policies is not only
potential, but is vigorously actual.

The person who wants Director status on the RCD
is presently an Associate (non-voting) Director. [See
attached for explanation of Associate Directors and what
they do.] Since an Associate can influence policy by
virtue of the respect accorded their special
association with the Board, does not that also constitute
an incompatibility of office?

Should you desire further information, please let
me know.

Sincerely yours,

Bd Wheeler

JUN - 1992

KAY S CENICEROS
3rd District Supervisor
County of Riverside
Melba Hoffman, Administrative Assistant
Jessie Myers, Administrative Assistant
Fran Grey, Secretary

3rd District

May 18, 1992

Fair Political Practice Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Sir:

There has been a question of "incompatibility of office" concerning
some members and applicants on the Elsinore-Murrieta-Anza Resource
Conservation District.

One Director is also on the Anza Valley Municipal Advisory Council
which is advisory to the County Supervisor for the Third District.
Another is an applicant who is a member of the Flood Control
Zone 7 Board, which is advisory to the County Supervisors in their
role as Flood Control Commissioners. Both of these boards are
advisory in nature but could be construed to influence the
legislative decisions.

Please advise in writing the ruling involving these different
roles.

Sincerely

KAY CENICEROS, SUPERVISOR

By: Melba Hoffman
Administrative Assistant

MH:fg

P.O. Box 1359 • Riverside, California 92502-1359 • 714 275-1030

I can see this appears to be a mostly blank document with very faint horizontal lines dividing it into sections. The image quality and contrast make it difficult to discern any clear text content. There appear to be some very faint markings in the upper right corner, but they are not legible enough to transcribe with confidence.

If there is specific text you need transcribed from this document, please provide a higher contrast or higher resolution version, or clarify what content you're looking for.
